metadata.title: Peer Institution AI Governance Baseline
metadata.source: Hand-curated from each peer institution's public AI portals and policy pages.
metadata.purpose: Mirror of uc_ai_baseline.json for non-UC peer institutions. Lets the Brief tab anchor 'peer moves' claims to a verifiable peer field, and feeds the companion 'UC's position vs. the field' gap matrix.
metadata.notes: Seed v0.1.0: only a small set of high-confidence public-record fields per peer. Fields not yet verified appear as { value: false, source_id: 'inventory-gap' } so the gap matrix surfaces them honestly. Enrich over time as the peer-move feed surfaces new positions.

dimensions.governance: Formal governance structures, councils, committees, oversight bodies
dimensions.policy: Written use policies, guidelines, principles documents
dimensions.academic_integrity: GenAI-specific academic integrity policies and guidance
dimensions.infrastructure: Enterprise AI platforms, approved tools, technical deployments
dimensions.leadership: Named AI leadership roles (CAIO, CIO involvement, etc.)
dimensions.health_ai: Health-system-specific AI governance, oversight, clinical AI
dimensions.research: AI in research policy, IRB guidance, responsible research use
dimensions.training: AI literacy programs, training resources, education initiatives
dimensions.security: Data classification, security policies specific to AI tools

has_enterprise_ai_platform.notes: U-M GPT, Maizey, and U-M GPT Toolkit form U-M's enterprise generative AI platform, available to all U-M faculty, staff, and students. Launched at scale in 2024.

has_genai_policy.notes: Public GenAI use policy not yet inventoried for this baseline; verify before citing in the brief.

has_ai_council.notes: Formal AI council structure not yet inventoried; UW publishes guidance through multiple offices.

has_genai_policy.notes: UW Office of the Provost AI portal aggregates campus guidance and policies.
Not yet inventoried for this baseline.

has_ai_council.notes: Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence (HAI) anchors the university's AI research and policy convening function.
MIT Schwarzman College of Computing organizes AI research, education, and policy collaboration across the institute.

has_ai_research_strategy.notes: CMU AI organizes one of the longest-running and broadest university AI research portfolios; the dedicated portal anchors strategy and program directories.
